title: China-to-Ethiopia BESS Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: ECAE PVoC Import Conformity for BESS Products
rows.target_requirement: The Ethiopian Conformity Assessment Enterprise (ECAE) administers the PVoC programme under Regulation No. 549/2024, which expanded coverage to additional product categories in May 2024. BESS products, including battery modules, inverters, and system-level assemblies, may fall within regulated categories depending on power rating and product classification. Exporters should confirm the current PVoC scope for BESS with ECAE or an authorised inspection body (such as TÜV Rheinland or Cotecna) before shipment. Where in scope, a Certificate of Conformity (CoC) is required for customs clearance in Ethiopia.
target_standards: Ethiopian Conformity Assessment Enterprise (ECAE) PVoC programme
IEC 62619 and IEC 62933 series (safety and performance — referenced for BESS conformity evidence)
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese BESS products are typically documented against GB 44240-2024 (safety requirements for electrochemical energy storage stations) and GB/T 36276-2023 (lithium-ion battery for electric energy storage). These domestic standards provide technical evidence for due diligence but are not accepted as substitutes for the ECAE PVoC CoC at the Ethiopian port of import.
rows.gap: Before finalising the shipment plan, verify with ECAE or an authorised inspection body whether the BESS product category and power rating are in PVoC scope. If in scope, engage the inspection body for pre-export inspection in China, obtain the CoC, and include IEC 62619 and IEC 62933 test evidence in the application package.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for BESS product categories within ECAE PVoC scope; scope should be confirmed with ECAE or an authorised inspection body
mandatory_status.cn: No equivalent mandatory pre-export verification for the domestic Chinese market; exporters must comply with destination-country conformity requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Confirm PVoC scope for BESS before shipping. Where in scope, a CoC from an authorised body is required for Ethiopia customs clearance. IEC 62619 and IEC 62933 evidence strengthens the application.

source.publisher: Cotecna (ECAE-authorised inspection body, Ethiopia CoC services)

rows.requirement: IEC 62619 Battery Safety and IEC 62933 System Performance Evidence
rows.target_requirement: IEC 62619:2022 specifies safety requirements for secondary lithium cells and batteries for use in industrial applications, covering the battery pack and BMS. IEC 62933-2-1 and IEC 62933-5-2 address system-level safety and grid-integration safety for electrical energy storage systems. These standards are the internationally recognised framework for BESS safety and performance evidence referenced by ECAE inspection bodies and project owners in Ethiopia. Third-party test reports or certificates to IEC 62619 from an accredited laboratory are commonly required by project owners, EPC contractors, financiers, and insurers for Ethiopian BESS projects.
target_standards: IEC 62619:2022 — Secondary cells and batteries containing alkaline or other non-acid electrolytes — Safety requirements for secondary lithium cells and batteries for use in industrial applications
IEC 62933-2-1 — Electrical energy storage systems — Unit parameters and testing methods — Part 2-1: Safety requirements for grid-integrated electrical energy storage systems
IEC 62933-5-2 — Electrical energy storage systems — Safety requirements for grid-integrated electrical energy storage systems — Particular requirements for lithium-ion-based systems
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese BESS manufacturers typically document battery safety against GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023. Where the manufacturer also holds an IEC 62619 report from an accredited laboratory, that report directly supports the Ethiopia project file. GB 44240-2024 alone is not treated as equivalent to IEC 62619 by international project owners or ECAE inspection bodies.
china_standards: IEC 62619 (where held by the Chinese manufacturer)
rows.gap: Obtain a current IEC 62619 test report or certificate from an accredited laboratory for the specific battery cell and pack configuration. For system-level projects, prepare IEC 62933 series evidence covering BMS, thermal management, and grid-integration safety. Confirm scope covers the Ethiopia market configuration before relying on an existing certificate.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary as standalone standards but practically required by ECAE inspection bodies, project owners, financiers, and insurers for BESS projects in Ethiopia
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 are the domestic mandatory/recommended equivalents; IEC 62619 is voluntary in China but required for many export markets

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] IEC 62619 battery safety evidence and IEC 62933 system-level evidence are practically required for Ethiopia BESS project acceptance and PVoC clearance support. GB 44240-2024 alone is not sufficient for international project and financing review.

rows.requirement: UN 38.3 Transport Testing for Lithium Battery BESS Shipments
rows.target_requirement: UN 38.3 (Part III, Section 38.3 of the UN Manual of Tests and Criteria) specifies eight tests for lithium metal and lithium-ion cells and batteries to be transported safely by air, sea, or land. Passing UN 38.3 is mandatory for international transport of all lithium batteries under the IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations (DGR), IMDG Code, and ADR. Since January 2020, a UN 38.3 Test Summary document must be provided along the supply chain. BESS lithium battery modules and cells shipped from China to Ethiopia must comply with the applicable dangerous-goods transport regulations for the chosen shipping mode.
target_standards: UN Manual of Tests and Criteria — Part III, Section 38.3 (UN 38.3)
IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations (DGR) — lithium battery provisions
IMDG Code — Class 9 dangerous goods, lithium battery packing instructions
ADR — dangerous goods road transport, where applicable for inland segments
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese lithium battery manufacturers exporting BESS products internationally are expected to hold UN 38.3 test reports from accredited laboratories. China's domestic GB/T 36276-2023 and GB 44240-2024 cover cell and system safety but do not substitute for UN 38.3 transport testing required by international dangerous-goods transport regulations.
china_standards: UN 38.3 (required for international transport regardless of domestic standard)
rows.gap: Obtain and retain UN 38.3 test reports and the mandatory Test Summary for each lithium cell and battery type in the BESS shipment. Ensure the shipping documentation, labelling, packing, and SDS comply with IATA DGR or IMDG Code for the chosen transport mode. Provide the Test Summary to all parties in the supply chain as required since January 2020.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for international transport of lithium batteries under IATA DGR, IMDG Code, and ADR; applies to all BESS lithium cell and battery shipments from China to Ethiopia regardless of destination-country standards
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for Chinese exporters shipping lithium batteries internationally under the same IATA, IMDG, and ADR transport regulations

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] UN 38.3 transport testing is mandatory for all lithium battery BESS shipments to Ethiopia by air, sea, or land. Obtain and distribute the Test Summary along the supply chain. Non-compliance risks shipment rejection, dangerous-goods penalty, or cargo detention.

source.publisher: JJR Laboratory — UN 38.3 Lithium Battery Shipping Compliance Guide

rows.requirement: Ethiopian Energy Authority Grid Approval for BESS Connected to the EEU Network
rows.target_requirement: BESS projects connected to the Ethiopian Electric Utility (EEU) distribution network or the Ethiopian Electric Power (EEP) transmission network require Ethiopian Energy Authority (EEA) licensing and EEU/EEP technical review. The Ethiopia National Distribution Grid Code (ENDGC) and Ethiopia National Transmission Grid Code (ENTGC) set technical connection requirements including voltage, frequency (50 Hz), protection co-ordination, control interface, and safety. Battery storage systems that provide grid services, capacity firming, or arbitrage connected to the EEU network must satisfy these requirements in addition to product-level safety certification.
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese BESS grid-connection evidence is based on GB/T 36558-2023 (general technical requirements for battery energy storage connected to power system) and utility-specific EMS and protection settings. These records address Chinese grid requirements and do not establish EEA or EEU connection approval for Ethiopian projects.
china_standards: Chinese utility EMS and protection parameter sheets
rows.gap: Engage EEA and EEU early for BESS project licensing and grid-connection review. Prepare Ethiopia-specific technical documentation: 50 Hz grid settings, protection co-ordination studies, BMS control interface, EMS integration plan, commissioning and testing plan, and site-specific safety assessment. Product-level certificates do not replace EEA project licensing or EEU connection agreement.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for BESS projects connected to or providing services to the EEU distribution network or EEP transmission network; off-grid or behind-the-meter systems may follow different requirements
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese domestic grid approval is not accepted as EEA or EEU connection approval for Ethiopia projects

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] BESS projects connected to the EEU grid require separate EEA licensing and EEU connection review. Product-level safety certificates address import clearance, not project-level grid approval. Build an Ethiopia-specific project connection pack.

source.publisher: ICLG — Renewable Energy Laws and Regulations Report 2026: Ethiopia
